title: hemizonia congesta subsp. luzulifolia
intro: Hemizonia congesta subsp. luzulifolia, also known as San Mateo tarweed, is a subspecies of the Hemizonia congesta plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is native to California. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs. The health benefits of Hemizonia congesta subsp. luzulifolia include its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to act as an expectorant, helping to clear mucus from the lungs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat respiratory issues, or as an essential oil, which can be inhaled to help ease congestion.
